sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: PAR SEC Filings - Par Technology 10-K, 10-Q, 8-K Forms

feed.title: One Stop Systems (OSS) Swings To Q3 Profit Challenging Longstanding Loss Narrative
feed.summary: One Stop Systems (OSS) reported a mixed Q3 2025, swinging to a small profit of US$0.3 million after five quarters of losses, with revenue reaching US$18.8 million. Despite this recent profitability, the company still faces a trailing 12-month loss of approximately US$6.9 million, indicating that long-term profitability is not yet established. Analysts and investors are closely watching whether the positive margin trends can be sustained given the company's past volatility and reliance on lumpy government contracts.

feed.title: Owens Corning Residential Insulation: Innovations Driving Energy Efficiency in 2026 Housing Boom
feed.summary: Owens Corning has launched enhanced residential insulation lines, including PINK Fiberglas with higher R-values and moisture resistance, to meet the EU's tightened 2026 energy efficiency mandates. These innovations position Owens Corning to gain significant market share in the DACH region, where increasing energy costs and government grants are fueling a €12 billion retrofit market. The new products offer superior thermal performance, fire safety, and sustainability, leading to strong investor interest.

This insider buying occurred despite the company missing quarterly earnings expectations, reporting a negative EPS, and having a high dividend yield with a negative payout ratio. Analysts currently have an average "Reduce" rating on the stock with an average target price of $47.50.

feed.title: Class action targets Costco over tariff refunds to members
feed.summary: A new federal class action lawsuit has been filed against Costco Wholesale Corp. in Puerto Rico, alleging the retailer is improperly retaining over $20 million in tariff refunds that customers paid through elevated prices. The lawsuit claims Costco raised prices on imported goods in 2025 to cover tariffs later struck down, and is now seeking to keep the refunds and interest rather than reimbursing members. Plaintiff Guillermo J. Ortiz is seeking restitution and class certification for all affected Costco members in Puerto Rico.

feed.title: Nuvve announces new 40MW Austrian BESS project
feed.summary: Nuvve Holding Corp. has partnered with OMNIA Group Holdings to add a 40MW / 80MWh Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) project in Austria, developed through Energiepark Graphit GmbH. This project is part of a joint European energy platform, expanding on their existing Swedish BESS project and a deployment in Denmark. Nuvve will provide consulting services and serve as the grid aggregation operator, earning a 9% aggregation fee on revenues.

(WMT-Q) to make way for better opportunities in her view. Tozser's equity portfolio is diversified across energy, U.S. technology, and U.S. and Canadian financials.

feed.title: FCC Greenlights $3.5B Tegna Sale to Nexstar Despite State Pushback
feed.summary: The FCC has approved Nexstar's $3.5 billion acquisition of Tegna, a significant step forward for the media deal despite objections from several states. While federal antitrust concerns have been cleared, state-level opposition remains, focusing on potential media consolidation risks and impact on local news diversity. This creates an "unpriced regulatory risk" for investors, making the deal's final timeline and valuation uncertain, even as Nexstar aims to strengthen its position in local broadcasting and digital distribution.

feed.title: Unilever and Kraft Heinz previously discussed food merger, reports suggest
feed.summary: Reports suggest that Unilever and Kraft Heinz previously discussed a merger that would have combined Kraft Heinz's condiments with Unilever's food business, potentially creating a new multi-billion dollar entity. These talks, which have since ended, occurred before Kraft Heinz's demerger was halted, aligning with Unilever's strategy to shift focus towards beauty and personal care and offload food brands. Both companies have declined to comment on the matter.

feed.title: The Travelers Companies, Inc. stock faces pressure after heavy insider selling wave amid stable insurer sector outlook
feed.summary: The Travelers Companies, Inc. (TRV) stock experienced downward pressure after top executives sold over $40 million in shares in late May 2025, raising questions about internal confidence despite strong quarterly results and a stable property-casualty insurance market. This insider selling, led by CEO Alan Schnitzer and CFO Daniel Frey, occurred soon after Travelers reported a 20.95% return on equity and $10.86 billion in revenue. While analysts note the robust financials and strong peer comparison of TRV, the timing of the sales prompted scrutiny for DACH investors who often look to US insurers for stable yields.
